name: binwalk (Firmware Analysis Tool)

auth.notes: Local tool; analyzes local firmware files

performance.notes: Scan time proportional to firmware size; entropy analysis adds overhead

agent_readiness.notes: Firmware analysis and extraction tool. CLI primary interface. binwalk firmware.bin. binwalk -e firmware.bin (extract). binwalk -E firmware.bin (entropy). Python API: import binwalk. for module in binwalk.scan('firmware.bin', signature=True, quiet=True): for result in module.results: print(result.offset, result.description). binwalk.scan('firmware.bin', extract=True). Results: FileType, compression, filesystem, bootloader signatures. Dependencies: binutils, jefferson, sasquatch, unstuff for full extraction. Entropy analysis for crypto/compression detection. Magic signatures database.

gotchas: Full extraction requires many external tools (sasquatch, jefferson, squashfs-tools) — complex setup
Python API less stable than CLI — binwalk 3.x rewrites may break Python bindings
False positives in signature scanning — verify results manually
No type annotations — limited IDE support
Filesystem extraction may overwrite files if run in wrong directory
best_when: IoT firmware security analysis — extracting filesystems, finding embedded files
Identifying embedded file systems, bootloaders, certificates in firmware
Entropy analysis to detect encryption or compression regions
Security research on embedded device firmware
avoid_when: General binary parsing (use construct or Kaitai Struct)
Simple file type detection (use python-magic instead)
When ReFirmLabs Centrifuge API is available (hosted, more powerful)
summary: binwalk analyzes and extracts firmware images. binwalk -e firmware.bin (CLI). Python: binwalk.scan('firmware.bin', signature=True). Signature detection for filesystems, compressors, bootloaders. Entropy analysis. MIT by ReFirmLabs. Best for IoT firmware security analysis. Complex extraction deps (sasquatch, jefferson). Python API less stable than CLI. False positives require manual verification. Use construct for structured binary parsing.
